action.reasoning: Observation: Iter 17 training completed with val_AP 0.0242, loss NaN, test AP 0.0254. Analysis: NaN loss arises because BCEWithLogitsLoss produces NaN for NaN labels, and multiplying by mask (0 * NaN) yields NaN. This poisons gradients, preventing learning. Fix: zero out unlabeled entries before summing. Also, low AP may require richer features (RDKit fingerprints). Decision: Fix train.py NaN masking, retrain 30 epochs to see if AP improves. If AP remains low, then enhance with fingerprint features.
